delete-branch.description: Deletes a local or remote branch
commands: git branch -d <branch>
git branch -D <branch>  # force delete even if not merged
git push origin --delete <branch>  # delete remote branch

undo-commit.description: Removes or undoes the last commit
commands: git reset --soft HEAD~1  # removes commit, keeps changes staged
git reset --hard HEAD~1  # removes commit and changes (destructive)
git revert HEAD  # creates a new commit that undoes the previous one (safe for remote)

remove-file.description: Removes a file from the repo or stops tracking it
commands: git rm <file>
git rm --cached <file>  # stops tracking but keeps it on disk

discard-changes.description: Discards uncommitted changes in the working directory
commands: git checkout -- <file>
git restore <file>  # modern equivalent
git reset --hard  # discards ALL uncommitted changes
git log --oneline --graph --all
git log -p <file>  # history of a specific file

rename-branch.description: Changes the name of a branch
commands: git branch -m <old> <new>
git branch -m <new>  # if you're on the branch you want to rename

stash-changes.description: Saves uncommitted changes to come back to them later
commands: git stash
git stash pop  # brings the stashed changes back
git stash list  # view all saved stashes
git rebase -i HEAD~<n>  # choose 'squash' for commits you want to combine

install-package.description: Installs a package as a project dependency
commands: npm install <package>
npm install <package> --save-dev  # as a development dependency only

install-global.description: Installs a package available globally on your machine
commands: npm install -g <package>

uninstall-package.description: Removes a package from the project
commands: npm uninstall <package>

update-packages.description: Updates packages according to the package.json limits
commands: npm update
npm outdated  # check which ones are outdated before updating

clear-cache.description: Cleans the npm cache (useful when installs are acting weird)
commands: npm cache clean --force

run-script.description: Runs a script defined in package.json
commands: npm run <script-name>
npm start  # shortcut if the script is named 'start'
npm init -y  # with defaults, no questions asked

check-vulnerabilities.description: Checks for known vulnerabilities in your dependencies
commands: npm audit
npm audit fix  # attempts to auto-fix what it can
